text: The surgeon incises the Gerota's fascia, following the psoas muscle towards the renal hilum, revealing underlying anatomical structures.
The DaVinci XI robotic system is utilized in the nephrectomy procedure, with four robotic arms actively engaged in the surgical field.
The surgeon uses a vessel loop to control the posterior branch of the vein and two arteries, ensuring vascular management during the nephrectomy.
The surgeon manages the adipose tissue to improve visibility in the operating field, ensuring clear access to the anatomical structures involved in the nephrectomy.
The surgeon uses the DaVinci XI system to carefully free the four-centimeter hilar tumor from the pelvic region, ensuring separation from nearby vascular structures and the urinary tract.
The surgeon frees the posterior plane, revealing the anterior branch of the vein, using the DaVinci XI system with four robotic arms.
The surgeon clips a small artery supplying the tumor and continues the posterior dissection to separate the tumor from the adjacent vein.
The surgeon performs a nuclear resection of the tumor without clamping, carefully excising the tumor tissue while maintaining blood flow.
The surgeon places the excised tumor into a surgical specimen bag, ensuring containment for removal from the operating field.
The surgeon performs hemostasis using a 3-0 monochrome running suture, securing the tissue to control bleeding in the surgical field.
